The Ford Explorer is a spacious 7-seater SUV designed for families, powered by a 2.3L EcoBoost engine with automatic transmission. It includes advanced safety features like adaptive cruise control, lane keeping assist, and blind spot monitoring. It is ideal for long-distance travel and comfort.

The Ford Mustang is a high-performance coupe powered by a 5.0L V8 engine with manual transmission. It is designed for speed and driving pleasure, featuring sport mode, traction control, and performance exhaust for enhanced driving dynamics.

The Ford F-150 is a powerful pickup truck built for heavy-duty tasks and towing. It comes with a 3.5L EcoBoost engine, 4x4 drive capability, and trailer assist features, making it suitable for both work and rugged terrains.

The Ford Escape is a compact SUV designed for city driving and small families. It features hybrid fuel efficiency, a 1.5L EcoBoost engine, and smart parking assist, making it easy to maneuver in urban environments.

The Ford Ranger is a midsize pickup truck designed for off-road adventures and utility. It includes a diesel engine, terrain management system, and towing support, making it suitable for challenging environments.
The Ford Explorer requires an oil change every 10000 km, tire rotation every 8000 km, and brake inspection every 15000 km. It comes with a standard warranty of 3 years.
The Ford Mustang requires an oil change every 7000 km, tire rotation every 6000 km, and brake inspection every 12000 km. It comes with a 3-year warranty.
The Ford F-150 requires an oil change every 12000 km, tire rotation every 10000 km, and brake inspection every 20000 km. It includes a 5-year warranty.
The Ford Escape requires an oil change every 9000 km, tire rotation every 7000 km, and brake inspection every 14000 km. It comes with a 3-year warranty.
The Ford Ranger requires an oil change every 11000 km, tire rotation every 9000 km, and brake inspection every 18000 km. It comes with a 4-year warranty.
The engine warning light indicates a problem with the engine or emission system. Immediate inspection is recommended to avoid damage.
The battery warning light indicates an issue with the charging system. This may be due to a faulty alternator or battery.
The oil pressure warning indicates low oil pressure. Stop the vehicle immediately to prevent engine damage.
The brake warning light indicates a potential issue with the braking system or low brake fluid level.
